author: @fjafadfa5036

title: Wow! This USC Tour Guide Said "F**k It" And Decided to Give an Honest Tour of Campus
body: When I was 17 I visited two colleges in which part of their campus tours were exactly the same. I swear, EXACTLY the same word for word. Penn State and Virginia Tech both told me they had the most friendly mail man in the world called Mike or Dave or something. Apparently, if you ever forgot to send your mom a card on her birthday and showed up at the post office, Mike the mailman would first give you a short lecture about organization and then do you a favor by putting a "lost in mail" stamp on your envelope to make it look like it was sent on time. I can't recite it exactly but I remember both campus literally used the exact same script. The guide even phrased it by saying "I even heard that..." to make it sound like some secret inside scoop you could only get from a student.
author: @adamjung2867

title: if USC were honest #usc #college #collegeadmissions #collegestudytips #ucla
body:
Hey!

I’m a graduate from the 90s!!

It was still a prestigious school, even back then.

I believe USC was ranked 51st in the nation out of 1,500 colleges when I graduated…

Just not in the top 25 or so like today.

Unless you use Forbes where I think USC was 14 last year?

They’ve changed those rankings and downgraded them unfortunately so the rankings are a little diluted.

But ranking isn’t everything… I had some of the best times of my life while there!

title: i transferred out of USC and went to Cornell (my honest thoughts 1 year later)
body: I'm from Socal. Back in the days USC was my top choice, didn't get in, so I chose to go to UC Berkeley instead (to experience a different culture, as opposed to staying put at UCLA). My first semester at Cal made me realize I didn't like my time there, so I spent my second semester planning to transfer to Cornell (Up to this point I had NEVER been anywhere east of Las Vegas). Forgot why Cornell of all places, maybe the pictures looked nice lol. Then when my second year came I completely gave up on that idea and looked up how to study abroad instead. I ended up spending 5 years in college in total, 2 of which were spent studying abroad. Don't know what life would've been like if I transferred to Cornell, but I'm content with my choices
author: @night0merchant

title: i transferred out of USC and went to Cornell (my honest thoughts 1 year later)

Good luck wherever you go young lady!

title: University of Southern California (USC) Campus Tour
body: I went here for a year hurt my knee and my scholarship was pulled so back to Ohio. Graduated Ohio State but I really enjoyed my year at USC. The campus is really nice and its always had a chill vibe. Its not University of Spoiled Kids anymore, people from all walks of life go there.
author: @sonimbetterthanyou

title: University of Southern California (USC) Campus Tour
body: OMG this campus looks stunning on camera, I can imagine how beautiful it is to be there in person. The sad part is, I have the grades to get accepted to USC but my piano playing is not close to the level to get admitted to their program. I worked my butt off to become a great pianist but had very bad musical training. If only I studied with the right piano professor 10 years ago, I would have been admitted 8 years ago, only ifs, I hate the word if.

title: University of Southern California (USC) Campus Tour
body: Each year, USC is lowering the acceptance rate for admissions and it’s concerning me because it’s the only school I want to go to for journalism. In 2019 11% of applicants were only accepted. The lowest in USC history. However, a large chunk of the applicants accepted are from CA community colleges which is where I’m going in the meantime for my GE.
author: @SoLA489

title: University of Southern California (USC) Campus Tour
body: Before you chose a university, check out a piece titled The Hatchet Man's Playbook.  There's a REALLY interesting chapter on the pluses & minuses of attending an elite university - and there are some surprising minuses.  For example, while attending an elite university can get you on the short lists of a few top-flight companies, it can also completely eliminate you from consideration from the vast majority of lesser employers whose managers are afraid you will take THEIR jobs if they hire you - an interesting bit of trivia nobody explained to me when I was university shopping.  Also, there's a tremendous tendency towards localism in the business community, something else I didn't know.  I thought the best candidate naturally got the job. (LOL!)  Not a chance.  Old-boy/alumni networks rule!  For example, at one time, something like 40% of the judges in CA were graduates of USC Law School - not Harvard, not Yale, not Stanford.  USC . ( I graduated from Irvine, BTW.) In Texas, the dominant schools are Baylor, A & M, & UT.  They basically run the state.  So before you choose a university, do your homework & see how strong the alumni associations are! At one time, Irvine's was run by a UCSB graduate, if that tells you anything.
